meta.purpose: Downloadable / scrapable sources of cognitive-aptitude / IQ test items (logic-first) for the Testmaker item bank, designer/generator, and renderer.

taxonomy.A1: Figure series (pick next figure in a sequence)

field_notes.access_class: downloadable-artifact | site-scrape | dataset-repo | api | interactive-only | generator
field_notes.license.category: public-domain | gov-public | open-source | open-cc | commercial-free-sample | commercial-paid | academic-restricted | unknown
field_notes.generator: true = produces unlimited items + documented rules (powers the designer/generator)
field_notes.ip_risk: low | medium | high (risk if items were reused/redistributed)
field_notes.priority: high | medium | low (value for a logic-first cognitive test bank)

sources.name: Open Matrices Item Bank (OMIB)
sources.provider: Koch, Spinath, Greiff & Becker (Saarland/Luxembourg) via OSF

extraction.notes: OSF project osf.io/4km79; download supplementary PNGs + pen-and-paper template. OSF has a REST API.

sources.notes: Best single grab for a matrix bank: validated, IRT difficulty from N=2572, construction rules documented for your generator. Share-alike (GPL) obligations apply.

sources.name: Matrix Reasoning Item Bank (MaRs-IB)
sources.provider: Chierchia, Blakemore et al.; Royal Society Open Science

license.detail: CC-BY article; items free for non-commercial use

extraction.notes: OSF osf.io/g96f4; item images + item-level CSV. Abstract-analogy 3x3 with rule-defined distractors.

sources.notes: Explicitly built as an open Raven's alternative. Non-commercial caveat - check before shipping commercially.

sources.name: International Cognitive Ability Resource (ICAR / ICAR16)
sources.provider: ICAR team (Condon, Revelle, Doebler, Holling) via PsychArchives

license.detail: Full bank under PsychArchives Scientific-Use License; ICAR16 sample items freely citable

extraction.notes: Full 1GB bank + automatic item generators behind a scientific-use gate; ICAR16 & matrix samples are in open-access papers (scrape figures).

sources.notes: Ships AUTOMATIC ITEM GENERATORS - directly relevant to the generator subsystem. Matrix items = images; letter-number-series & verbal = text.

sources.name: Sandia Matrices (norming set + Java generator)

extraction.notes: Download Matzen_et_al_2010_norming_stim.zip for normed item images + CSV. Java generator is dated.

sources.notes: One of very few truly free, NORMED, Raven-style sets from a reputable institution.

sources.name: Sandia Matrices - psychometric review supplementary tests
sources.provider: Harris et al. 2020, Personnel Assessment and Decisions (BGSU)

extraction.notes: Download article PDF + SupplementaryMaterials.zip (scoring keys). Pairs with sandialabs image repo.

sources.notes: Turns the raw Sandia generator into ready-to-administer short forms with keys.

sources.name: raven-matrix (Sandia SGMT Python port)

extraction.notes: Modern Python; `raven-matrix build --code A1B2C4 --png`. SVG-first output ideal for a renderer. Shell out from Go or port logic.

sources.notes: Most practical live matrix generator; carries Sandia normed difficulty. Top pick for the generator subsystem (safe BSD license).

sources.name: pyRavenMatrices

license.detail: No LICENSE file - treat as all-rights-reserved until clarified

extraction.notes: Cairo/pycairo; library-style rule composition. Good architecture reference.

sources.notes: Clean rule-composition API. Missing license blocks production use - verify with author.

sources.name: matRiks (R package)
sources.provider: Brancaccio, Epifania, Anselmi, de Chiusole (Univ. Padova)

extraction.notes: install.packages('matRiks'); rules span visuospatial->logic. Distractors modeled on empirical Raven error patterns; Rasch-validated difficulty.

sources.notes: Best-documented rule taxonomy + DIAGNOSTIC distractor logic. Tutorial paper is a design blueprint to port to Go.

sources.name: OpenPsychometrics - Matrix Reasoning IQ test + raw data

license.detail: Project releases data to public domain / CC0-style

extraction.notes: Download _rawdata/IQ1.zip (responses + codebook); scrape the 25 item PNGs from the live test pages.

sources.notes: Rare combo: free items + real response data + PD license. Author flags quality as 'experimental' - cross-check item difficulty from the data.

sources.name: OpenPsychometrics - Vocabulary IQ Test (VIQT)

extraction.notes: Items are text; scrape VIQT page or pull response dataset. OpenPsych paper flags weak items to drop.

sources.notes: High-quality, truly public-domain verbal filler for the C family.

sources.name: PsyToolkit Experiment Library

license.detail: Free for research; copy/modify/embed (per-experiment copyright page)

extraction.notes: Each experiment has a downloadable zip (script + stimuli): mental rotation, N-back, Corsi, digit-symbol, Deary-Liewald.

sources.notes: Best for SPEED / WORKING-MEMORY (E) & mental rotation (D1) mechanics and as a reference implementation of RT-scored timed tasks. No matrices.

sources.name: Yerkes 1921 - Army Alpha & Beta (Psychological Examining in the US Army)
sources.provider: US War Dept / NAS; digitized by Internet Archive
sources.item_count: Hundreds across Alpha (8) + Beta (7) tests + alternate forms

extraction.notes: 938-page book; PDF (59MB) or JP2 zip for clean plate images. Beta plates = figural images; Alpha = text via OCR. Items spread through document.

sources.notes: Canonical fully-PD historical source. Beta = free figural/spatial/speed; Alpha = verbal + arithmetic. Curate dated 1917 content.

sources.name: Wikimedia Commons - intelligence test figures

license.detail: PD or CC per-file (each file states its license)

sources.item_count: Dozens of illustrative figures (not full item sets)

extraction.notes: Use MediaWiki API (categorymembers + imageinfo) - category HTML is JS-rendered/empty. Verify each file's license individually.

sources.notes: Cleanest source of individually-licensed, safely-reusable example figures + a few genuine PD Army plate scans. Not a bulk item source.

sources.name: RAVEN dataset

extraction.notes: git clone + generate any N. Each item = PNG panels + XML rule annotation + npz. Legacy Py2.7.

sources.notes: Canonical figural-matrix dataset with full rule grammar (A-SIG). KNOWN answer-set shortcut bias - prefer I-RAVEN / RAVEN-FAIR for training/eval.

sources.name: I-RAVEN / SRAN (Impartial RAVEN)

extraction.notes: Attribute Bisection Tree builds balanced distractors that defeat the RAVEN shortcut. Same PNG+XML format, drop-in.

sources.notes: Preferred over vanilla RAVEN: removes answer-set bias, cleaner license.

sources.name: RAVEN-FAIR
sources.provider: Benny, Pekar, Wolf (Tel Aviv Univ.), CVPR 2021

extraction.notes: `python main.py --fair 1`. Different balanced-answer algorithm than I-RAVEN.

sources.notes: Second impartial variant; good for cross-checking distractor strategies.

sources.name: A-I-RAVEN / I-RAVEN-Mesh (raven-datasets)

extraction.notes: Modern Python 3.9, unit-tested. Flags to control color/position/size/type + mesh + held-out rules. Also on HuggingFace datasets.

sources.notes: Most maintained, most configurable RAVEN-lineage generator (2025). Best modern starting point for the matrix engine.

sources.name: PGM - Procedurally Generated Matrices
sources.provider: DeepMind (Barrett, Hill, Santoro et al.), ICML 2018

extraction.notes: tar.gz from GCS 'ravens-matrices' bucket (tens of GB) or tfds.load('abstract_reasoning'). No generator source released.

sources.notes: Largest keyed matrix corpus + best-documented [relation, object, attribute] rule schema. NON-COMMERCIAL - use as rule/format reference, do not ship items.

sources.name: DeepMind Visual Analogy (VAP)

extraction.notes: analogies/*.tar.gz in the same GCS bucket as PGM.

sources.notes: Best large figural-analogy corpus; same non-commercial restriction as PGM (format/rule model only).

sources.name: ARC-AGI-1 (Abstraction and Reasoning Corpus)

extraction.notes: Each task = 2-5 demo grid pairs + test input. Open-ended output grids (not MCQ) - convert to pick-the-output if needed.

sources.notes: Gold standard for abstraction; cleanest license. Source of transformation CONCEPTS (rotation, reflection, counting, symmetry) to adapt into matrix rules.

sources.name: ARC-AGI-2

extraction.notes: Same JSON schema as v1; harder, more compositional.

sources.notes: Hardest abstract-reasoning concepts, same permissive license/format as v1.

sources.name: ConceptARC

extraction.notes: JSON tasks + human/model performance CSVs. Concept-group labels = ready ontology of reasoning concepts.

sources.notes: Best for a CONCEPT TAXONOMY to structure difficulty ladders; includes human performance as pseudo-norms.

sources.name: ARC-GEN + ARC-GEN-100K

extraction.notes: Python generators reproduce/vary each ARC task; emit unlimited grid pairs matching original distributions.

sources.notes: Most faithful open procedural generator for ARC-style abstraction tasks.

sources.name: RE-ARC + ARC-DSL
sources.item_count: Unlimited per ARC task; DSL = 141 primitives

extraction.notes: Per-task generators + a 141-primitive DSL of grid ops (rotate, mirror, recolor, count, gravity).

sources.notes: The MIT-licensed ARC-DSL is a superb catalog of figural/grid transformation operations to reuse or port to Go for the rule engine.

sources.name: Machine Number Sense (MNS)

extraction.notes: pip install machine-number-sense; And-Or-Graph grammar composes layouts with number symbols -> PNG + label.

sources.notes: Supports the Numerical family (number sense / calculation) with a documented grammar generator. Same lab as RAVEN.

sources.name: Bongard-LOGO
sources.provider: NVIDIA (Nie, Yu, Mao et al.), NeurIPS 2020

extraction.notes: pip install bongard; turtle action-program generation (BongardProblem->Image->OneStrokeShape). Needs xvfb/ghostscript headless.

sources.notes: Premier open, MIT-licensed generator for ODD-ONE-OUT / classification figural items (A4). Invent unlimited original items.

sources.name: Bongard-HOI

license.detail: Code open; underlying photos have own licenses

extraction.notes: Uses natural photos (HAKE/HICO); requires external image downloads.

sources.notes: Poor fit - real photos, messier IP. Reasoning-benchmark reference only; skip for item generation.

sources.name: CLEVR / CLEVRER

extraction.notes: Blender-rendered 3D scenes + functional question programs. Heavy.

sources.notes: 3D-scene VQA, not figural matrices. Value = the functional-program -> guaranteed-correct-answer design pattern for your generator's answer-key guarantees.

sources.name: Number/letter-series generators & solvers (SeqSolver, sequence_generator)

extraction.notes: SeqSolver solves & validates number series (IST/PJP style); sequence_generator emits numeric/alpha/hex sequences. Lightweight, easy to port to Go.

sources.notes: Supports Numerical B3 + letter-series. SeqSolver doubles as an auto-verifier / answer-key generator in the pipeline.

sources.name: ReasonEra '47 datasets for abstract/logical/inductive reasoning' index

extraction.notes: Discovery hub to enumerate more downloadable figural/logical corpora; check each link's license.

sources.notes: Efficient way to find additional keyed, downloadable reasoning datasets beyond this catalog.

sources.name: Raven's Progressive Matrices (SPM/APM/CPM)
sources.provider: John C. Raven; published by Pearson

license.detail: STILL under Pearson copyright - NOT public domain

sources.item_count: SPM 60 / APM 48 / CPM 36 (cannot legally source real items)

extraction.notes: DO NOT scrape/redistribute real items. Leaked 'full SPM/APM' PDFs are infringing. Harvest only FORMAT from vendor practice pages.

sources.notes: The single biggest IP trap. Mirror the format via open banks (OMIB/MaRs-IB/Sandia/matRiks) instead of copying Raven items.

sources.name: Cattell Culture Fair Intelligence Test (CFIT / IPAT)

license.detail: Current CFIT copyrighted (Hogrefe/IPAT); floating manuals likely unauthorized

extraction.notes: No clean legal item download. Smithsonian leaflet + Wikipedia describe the 4 subtests (Series/Classification/Matrices/Conditions).

sources.notes: Format reference only (Series/Classification/Matrices/Conditions). Regenerate original items; don't copy circulating scans.

sources.name: Wonderlic (WPT) - third-party sample sets

license.detail: Third-party imitations; real WPT is copyrighted by Wonderlic Inc.

extraction.notes: 50-question PDFs with explanations; text, easy to parse. Look-alikes of uncertain provenance.

sources.notes: Format template only for a fast 50-item / 12-min mixed speeded section. Low priority for a logic-first bank.

sources.name: SHL - example questions + Verify practice tests

license.detail: (c) SHL; ToS threatens legal action for sharing results

sources.item_count: ~3-8 examples per type + full practice players

extraction.notes: Items load via /htmlLightbox/view/{id} JS + TalentCentral players - need Playwright/Puppeteer + screenshots.

sources.notes: Inductive = complete-the-5-box-sequence; verbal = T/F/cannot-say; Verify G+ = 24 items/36min tri-ability. FORMAT reference only - do not copy items.

sources.name: Aon / cut-e - official practice-task PDFs

extraction.notes: ~10 public PDFs on assets.aon.com (inductive scales, gapChallenge matrix, switchChallenge, numerical, verbal). Figure-heavy - render pages to images.

sources.notes: Best free/legit vendor source: Aon's OWN candidate materials, worked answers, covers A/B/C/E. Mirror formats; don't redistribute PDFs/figures.

sources.name: Thomas GIA - example booklet PDF

license.detail: (c) Thomas; must be reproduced in its entirety (no carving out items)

extraction.notes: Direct GET, no auth. Text extracts cleanly; spatial R-symbols + letter grids need image extraction.

sources.notes: Best direct-download in the vendor batch. Reusable = subtest MECHANICS (furthest-from-middle number, odd-word-out, rotate-symbol-vs-mirror).

sources.name: ACE Cognitive Test (Master International)
sources.item_count: A few illustrative items; bank proprietary

extraction.notes: GET brochure + fact-sheet PDFs (format + published norms). Live CAT demo email-gated + adaptive (no fixed item set).

sources.notes: 3x3 matrix format matches the A2/A3 focus; adaptive CAT correlates Raven's APM ~0.7. FORMAT + psychometric-design reference.

sources.name: Cubiks / Talogy - Logiks practice

extraction.notes: Examples in a Drupal JS widget; scrape embedded PNGs or render. Logiks General Intermediate = 50Q (V24/N16/A10) in 12 min.

sources.notes: Low yield (~10 items). Reusable = the three-section 12-min speeded General Ability format.

sources.name: Saville - Swift Analysis / Executive Aptitude practice
sources.item_count: A handful per section; Swift Analysis = 24 items

extraction.notes: JS Oasys engine, desktop-only, no publisher PDF. Diagrammatic/abstract need screenshot capture.

sources.notes: Reusable = speeded 3-section 24-item structure; diagrammatic 'missing figure in sequence' is most on-mission but hardest to capture.

sources.name: Eligo (PERLS) - practice-exercise demos

extraction.notes: start.php returns empty to plain HTTP - JS/AJAX engine. Render + screenshot; no answer key served. EXCLUDE the personality demo (iIID=27942).

sources.notes: Official vendor demos, cognitive subtests only. Higher engineering cost (JS, no keys) than Aon.

sources.name: PI Cognitive Assessment (PICA / PLI)

license.detail: (c) PI; no official free items - third-party samples only

extraction.notes: No official artifact. Third-party HTML samples are vendors' own copyrighted re-creations (ToS forbids scraping).

sources.notes: Mirror only the FORMAT: 50 items / 12 min, mixed numerical+verbal+abstract, increasing difficulty (PLI heritage).

sources.name: People Test Logic

extraction.notes: Only third-party re-creations exist (copyrighted, ToS forbids scraping).

sources.notes: Mirror only the FORMAT: 80 items / 30 min, three sections (logical/numerical/verbal), speeded.

sources.name: Matrigma (Assessio / Hogan) - Classic + Adaptive

extraction.notes: No official artifact; third-party samples copyrighted. FORMAT is the value - not the items.

sources.notes: Single most on-mission FORMAT: pure 3x3 one-missing-tile matrices, Classic-fixed vs Adaptive-branching. Mirror the transformation rules + adaptive delivery.

sources.name: Garuda Logic (cognitive part only)

extraction.notes: No free sample, demo, or PDF. Only marketing pages document the 3x10 / 20-min format.

sources.notes: Only the FORMAT is documented (30-item, 3-domain, 20-min). Ignore Garuda's personality profiles (out of scope).

sources.name: IST 2000-R (Intelligence Structure Test)

extraction.notes: No free samples; purchase requires proof of eligibility. Item-type descriptions only in academic papers.

sources.notes: Best FORMAT/taxonomy map to the project (maps ~1:1 to targeted test types incl. arithmetic-signs, cubes, matrices, memory). Architecture reference for composite speeded batteries.

sources.name: British Mensa Online Workout (official free practice)

extraction.notes: Registration-gated JS quiz; page HTML has only the form. Drive headless browser to capture image items + end answers.

sources.notes: Flagship reference FORMAT for the A3 Mensa-style focus (~45 items/20 min speeded homogeneous figure reasoning for the real supervised test). Mirror, don't copy.

sources.name: AssessmentDay

license.detail: (c) AssessmentDay; older numerical PDF = share-with-attribution/non-commercial; newer PDFs = no redistribution

extraction.notes: Un-gated PDFs; each test has a matching Solutions booklet with WORKED explanations. Stems = text; figures/graphs = embedded images (render + OCR/vision). Scrape topic .htm pages to enumerate current links.

sources.notes: HIGHEST-VALUE prep source: free PDFs WITH worked-solution booklets, no signup. Gold-standard template for logic-first families. Format reference (items copyrighted).

sources.name: Psychometric Success

extraction.notes: Un-gated PDFs at /test-pdfs/ with printed answer keys (letters only). Filenames: PsychometricSuccess<Type>-PracticeTest1.pdf. Numerical/verbal text extracts; abstract/spatial figures need OCR. Also mirrored on Scribd.

sources.notes: Easiest bulk grab: clean filenames, ~25 items/paper, printed keys. Pairs with AssessmentDay (which adds worked explanations).

sources.name: Practice4Me

extraction.notes: Free CCAT + numerical PDFs on /wp-content/uploads/ with EXPLAINED answers. Numerical PDF has a no-spaces text quirk (handle run-together tokens).

sources.notes: Free CCAT + numerical PDFs with explanations. Good for CCAT-style speeded mixed-ability items.

sources.name: 123test - IQ + Culture-Fair tests

extraction.notes: Figure/cube/matrix items are individual .gif assets at predictable paths; correct answer is server-side (no key). Full HTML exposes all stems.

sources.notes: Best free example of a full mixed-family IQ test with real IQ scoring + norms + 11 languages. No answer key is the limitation.

sources.name: Fibonicci

extraction.notes: JS-rendered (plain fetch empty); number-series & syllogisms are TEXT (no OCR). Answers/explanations after submit. Scribd mirrors are a static fallback.

sources.notes: EXCELLENT for number-series (B3) + syllogisms (A5) as pure TEXT with graded answers + easy/medium/hard tiers - avoids the OCR problem.

sources.name: Practice Aptitude Tests
sources.item_count: Large catalog but free tier = 1 test/type behind free account

extraction.notes: Free interactive tests need signup; solutions render client-side. Best scraped via the public sample-Q&A article (answers inline).

sources.notes: Big catalog but mostly gated; only thin free samples openly scrapable.

sources.name: JobTestPrep
sources.item_count: A few free sample items/topic; PDFs are guides not banks

extraction.notes: Items paywalled; 3 free guide PDFs (incl. SHL scoring guide). Free sample items are interactive HTML.

sources.notes: Not a good item source (paywalled). Value = the free SHL Results/scoring guide for the percentile/IQ-band scoring feature.

sources.name: iPREP
sources.item_count: ~10-25 sample items per course x 30+ courses

extraction.notes: Free samples on each /courses/<test>/ page with worked explanations + videos. Full sims paid.

sources.notes: Strong for branded-test FORMAT reference (CCAT, PI Cognitive, Wonderlic, Thomas GIA) with explanations; weak for bulk extraction.

sources.name: WikiJob free tests

extraction.notes: Free, no signup; answers+explanations appended after 10 items. Numerical/verbal = text; diagrammatic/abstract = images. Overlaps Psychometric Success (same owner).

sources.notes: Decent free interactive bank with end-of-test explanations; best for numerical/verbal text items.

sources.name: Assessment-Training.com
sources.item_count: 3 free tests (~40-50 items); 206 tests / 3,281 Q behind paywall

extraction.notes: Free tests launch via JS widget loading from an internal API (inspect XHR/JSON). Only 3 free tests extractable.

sources.notes: Rich topic taxonomy (syllogisms, odd-one-out, Venn, Raven's) useful as a category map; almost everything paywalled.

sources.name: GraduateMonkey
sources.item_count: Minimal free; core = paid tutorial packs

extraction.notes: No free item PDF; packs are paid.

sources.notes: High-quality SHL/Saville-style inductive + diagrammatic FORMAT, but paywalled - format reference only.

sources.name: Graduates First

license.detail: Free via university SSO; else paid

sources.item_count: 150+ tests but gated behind university email

extraction.notes: Free access needs an institutional email; not openly scrapable at scale.

sources.notes: High-quality (SHL-lineage) but effectively gated; not a practical open source.

sources.name: TestHQ / TestGorilla / adjacent
sources.item_count: Scattered free samples in study guides

extraction.notes: Thin free layers; scrape sample questions embedded in guides. TestGorilla has no public free bank.

sources.notes: Round out branded-format coverage only; low yield.

sources.name: Mensa Norway IQ Test (test.mensa.no)

license.detail: Mensa(R) trademark; (c) 2023 author - free to take, NOT to redistribute

extraction.notes: 35 question + 210 answer PNGs are individually addressable on static.mensa.no. Key is NOT in the DOM (server-scored) - solve manually or leave blank.

sources.notes: The most-cloned free figural IQ test online; canonical Matrigma/Raven-style specimen for the PRIMARY family. Format/style reference only.

sources.name: Mensa International IQ Challenge

extraction.notes: Redirects to the Mensa Norway engine - extract from test.mensa.no instead.

sources.notes: Duplicate of Mensa Norway under the international brand (multilingual). Cross-reference only.

sources.name: Mensa Denmark / iqtest.dk Culture-Fair Test

license.detail: Mensa(R) + author copyright; answers explicitly NOT permitted to publish

extraction.notes: Shape PNGs directly addressable at /iqtest/shapes/qN.png; answers deliberately withheld + legally protected from republication.

sources.notes: Second canonical culture-fair figural matrix set; historically one of the most-taken free IQ tests. Format reference only.

sources.name: American Mensa Home Test (MHT)

extraction.notes: Paywalled; no scrapable items.

sources.notes: Cataloged for completeness; not usable as an item source.

sources.name: Paul Cooijmans - High-Range IQ Tests (GliaWeb / Giga Society)

license.detail: Original author IP; free to take, contribution-based scoring, answers must NOT be shared

extraction.notes: Test pages return near-empty HTML - items are order-gated. Register + order (voluntary contribution) to receive the paper; redistribution prohibited.

sources.notes: Notable free-ORIGINAL-IP case, but mostly verbal/numerical/spatial (little pure figural-matrix); items not cleanly downloadable. Style/norms reference. Cite only the authentic domains (impostor 'Giga Society' clones exist).

sources.name: Xavier Jouve - Cogn-IQ JCTI / JCFS
sources.item_count: Adaptive ~30 items from a calibrated pool

extraction.notes: Adaptive CAT - item shown depends on prior answers, so no single-pass static bank. Free, no signup, instant results.

sources.notes: High-quality, genuinely figural/inductive, strong psychometrics (alpha .96). Adaptive delivery limits bulk extraction. Sibling JCFS = grid figural sequences.

sources.name: IQ Comparison Site - Advanced Culture Fair IQ Test

license.detail: EXTREMELY aggressive IP terms ($1000/item republish penalty)

extraction.notes: Only 1 sample GIF scrapable; 36 scored items behind signup+payment. DO NOT scrape/republish.

sources.notes: Avoid for items (hostile IP). The site's IQ-tables/percentile/norms reference pages are useful free non-item content.

sources.name: Free figural IQ sites (iqtestfree.org etc.)

license.detail: No stated license; assume site copyright

extraction.notes: Client-side rendered; results in localStorage, keys not exposed. LOW quality.

sources.notes: Entertainment-grade, optimized for ads not psychometrics. Extra style examples only - not for a serious bank.

sources.name: IndiaBIX

license.detail: (c) IndiaBIX; free to view, not open-licensed

extraction.notes: Predictable URL pagination /{section}/{topic}/{NNNNNN}. Plain fetch returns Q+options+answer+explanation as TEXT (no JS/auth). Non-verbal items are figure images (need OCR/labeling); text families scrape cleanly.

sources.notes: HIGHEST-VOLUME scrapable bank: every item has answer + explanation, clean pagination, covers ALL families. (c) IndiaBIX - internal research / seed original items, don't redistribute verbatim.

sources.name: Sawaal

extraction.notes: Topic pages with count in slug; pagination via ?page=. Text families scrape as text; figural = images. Dedupe against IndiaBIX.

sources.notes: Second big free bank with answers+explanations; cross-fill topics IndiaBIX is thin on.

sources.name: GeeksforGeeks - Aptitude & Reasoning

extraction.notes: Article-per-topic; clean static HTML, scrapes as text. Figures embedded.

sources.notes: Third India-style bank; smaller/less systematic but well-explained. Supplementary cross-fill.

sources.name: Hitbullseye / Smartkeeda / Testbook (India aptitude aggregators)

license.detail: (c) respective sites; some behind free signup

extraction.notes: Mostly static topic pages; Testbook gates full sets behind free account + per-topic PDF downloads.

sources.notes: Redundant with IndiaBIX/Sawaal; useful to diversify item phrasing.

sources.name: ASVAB - Official Sample Questions

license.detail: US federal government work - public domain (despite site footer)

extraction.notes: Per-subtest slugs; JS 'select the answer' widget but Q+options+key are in delivered HTML. Also grab /researchers/norming-information/ for the AFQT percentile model.

sources.notes: GENUINELY PUBLIC DOMAIN. Low volume - value = authoritative reference items + a PD scoring/norming (AFQT percentile) model you can legally mirror.

sources.name: AFOQT - Official Information Pamphlet

license.detail: US federal government work - public domain

sources.item_count: ~55 items (5 per subtest x 12) + full key

extraction.notes: Single direct PDF. Verbal/math subtests extract as text; spatial (Rotated Blocks, Block Counting, Hidden Figures) are figure images. Answer key = plain text (p.27).

sources.notes: HIGH VALUE among gov sources: PUBLIC DOMAIN and covers the hard-to-source SPATIAL families (mental rotation, 3-D counting, hidden figures, perceptual speed) WITH a key.

sources.name: UK Civil Service - official practice questions

license.detail: UK Crown copyright - reusable under Open Government Licence v3 (attribution)

sources.item_count: 4 numerical + 4 verbal worked; guides add more

extraction.notes: Direct PDF, pure text, trivial to extract Q+options+answer+explanation. Candidate-guide PDFs add examples.

sources.notes: Low volume but genuinely reusable (OGL v3, attribution). Items on-mission: number series, arithmetic signs, syllogism, analogies, antonyms, T/F/cannot-say.

sources.name: EPSO / EU Careers - official sample reasoning tests

license.detail: EU institutional material - reuse generally permitted with attribution; Scribd mirrors are third-party

extraction.notes: Official samples JS-driven (headless browser). Easier bulk = legacy 'Abstract/Numerical/Verbal Reasoning Sample Tests - EU EPSO' PDFs mirrored on SlideShare/Scribd (figural items + answers).

sources.notes: On-mission figural ABSTRACT reasoning (A1/A2/A3) PLUS non-English (FR/DE) items - rare. High strategic value for figural + multilingual.

sources.name: GRE - Official Sample Questions & Practice

license.detail: (c) ETS; free to download for personal practice, not open-licensed

sources.item_count: ~dozen worked/type + full practice book + 2 POWERPREP tests

extraction.notes: Direct PDFs, text-extractable with explanations. POWERPREP is proprietary software (not scrapable).

sources.notes: Authoritative verbal + quantitative items with rich explanations + a documented 130-170 scaled-score/percentile model to emulate. Copyrighted - internal reference/format only.

sources.name: GMAT - Official Starter Kit
sources.item_count: ~70 sample Q + 2 full adaptive exams (free tier)

extraction.notes: Account-gated proprietary adaptive software; needs authenticated session. Not a bulk pull.

sources.notes: Best ADAPTIVE-delivery + percentile-scoring reference (directly relevant to the adaptive/scoring requirements). Architectural reference, not item harvesting.

sources.name: UCAT - Official Practice Tests

extraction.notes: JS single-page app (plain fetch returns empty) - needs browser automation. Decision Making (logic/syllogism) is most on-mission.

sources.notes: CORRECTION: UCAT REMOVED Abstract Reasoning (figural) in 2025 - no longer a figural source. Still good for Decision-Making logic + T/F/cannot-say verbal. Legacy AR banks circulate on third-party mirrors.

sources.name: UK 11+ Non-Verbal Reasoning - free practice papers
sources.provider: Aggregators + publishers (CGP, Bond, Collins, GL Assessment)

license.detail: Mixed: publisher samples (c) CGP/Bond/Collins/GL; aggregator rehosts murky. Treat as copyrighted

sources.item_count: Large in aggregate (dozens of NVR full papers)

extraction.notes: Direct PDFs (mostly no login); figures = images needing page-extraction + rule labeling. Answer sheets separate. Harvest links then fetch each.

sources.notes: HIGH VALUE for the PRIMARY figural focus at scale (figure series, matrices, odd-one-out, mirror/rotation, paper-folding). Reverse-engineer transformation rules; avoid verbatim reuse.

sources.name: SSC (CGL/CHSL) Reasoning - previous-year papers
sources.provider: Staff Selection Commission (Govt of India) + aggregators

license.detail: Govt-of-India exam questions + aggregator-added solutions (c) aggregator

sources.item_count: Hundreds of papers (~25 reasoning Q each), 2010-2025

extraction.notes: Bulk free PDFs (bilingual en/hi). Topic-wise solved compilations are most data-friendly. Text extracts; figural = images.

sources.notes: Very large bank of REAL standardized-government reasoning items with keys, bilingual. Questions from an official body; PDFs aggregator-hosted with proprietary solutions - reference/format source.

sources.name: CCAT (Criteria Cognitive Aptitude Test) - free prep samples

license.detail: Real CCAT (c) Criteria Corp; vendor samples (c) vendor

extraction.notes: Official interactive-only; vendors offer free 30-50-item mocks (some printable PDFs). Items are vendor-created CCAT-style.

sources.notes: Speeded mixed-family model (50Q/15min, verbal+math/logic+spatial/abstract) - useful mechanics reference. Same pattern as Wonderlic (proprietary; only imitations free).
